title: Iran attacks oil tankers passing through Strait of Hormuz | ABC NEWS
description: Dramatic scenes have unfolded in the Strait of Hormuz in the Persian Gulf, where about one fifth of the world's oil passes through, as Iran attacks oil tankers and cargo vessels.   As the US and Israel's war with Iran continues to escalate, thirty two countries have agreed to release four hundred million barrels of oil to address fears over global supply. title: Fmr ASIO boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission | ABC NEWS
description: Dennis Richardson has resigned from the government's royal commission into antisemitism just weeks before the deadline for its interim report, saying he felt he was "grossly overpaid" and no longer required. The former ASIO boss was initially contracted to lead a narrower review into intelligence and security agencies, but that work was later folded into the royal commission process.  Members of the opposition have said the prime minister must convince Richardson to stay on the inquiry. title: Kneecap rapper will not face terror trial over accused Hezbollah flag | ABC NEWS
description: Kneecap rapper Liam Óg Ó hAnnaidh — who performs as Mo Chara — won't face a trial on terror-related charges.  The rapper was accused of flying a flag for Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, which is banned in Britain as a terrorist organisation, at a London gig in November 2024.  Judges at the High Court in London rejected an appeal from the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) against the decision to throw out the case last September after a court ruled it had originally been brought without the permission of the Director of Public Prosecutions and the Attorney General, and also one day outside the six-month statutory limit.  The CPS said it would challenge the ruling and London's High Court rejected its appeal on Wednesday.  The three-member band, which hails from Belfast, Northern Ireland, has faced criticism for political statements seeming to glorify militant groups including Hamas and Hezbollah.  "Let us be unequivocal: we do not, and have never, supported Hamas or Hezbollah," they said previously in a statement.  "We condemn all attacks on civilians, always. It is never OK."  When Kneecap performed in Melbourne, they attracted controversy after bringing the missing head of a statue of King George V on stage.  Canada and Hungary have previously banned Kneecap.
